scenario: Apr 7 8pm ET deadline tonight; 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait; Iran proposal rejected; IDF strikes Iran's largest facility; AIS transits -95%

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Fertilizer cost pass-through: Gulf urea still +90% since Hormuz closure. 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait Apr 6; Apr 7 deadline tonight reinforces no supply chain resolution. Egypt panic-buying continues; Apr 7 binary outcome is next catalyst for Asia rice-belt planting-season supply risk. Source: Barchart ZWK26.

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Biofuel demand elevated as jet fuel tracks Brent near four-year highs. Indonesian ethanol mandate acceleration competes with feed use. 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait Apr 6; Apr 7 deadline tonight; fertilizer cost uplift on next planting cycle persists; Tehran Toll Booth active.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Bunker cost elevated as Brent holds near four-year highs ahead of Apr 7 deadline. Indonesian and Vietnamese crush margins under continued pressure from elevated freight. Limited direct Hormuz exposure - US/South America origin - caps upside vs energy commodities but freight overhang persists.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: Crude oil correlation holding as Brent near four-year highs ahead of Apr 7 deadline. Biodiesel feedstock demand elevated; Indonesia B40 mandate intact. Fertilizer disruption threatening next Indonesian yield cycle. 15 US soldiers injured Kuwait Apr 6; IDF strikes Iran's largest facility; no near-term resolution signal. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD.

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: ASEAN food security alarm: fertilizer shortfall threatens Vietnam and Thailand rice belts. Philippines, Indonesia importing at distressed freight premiums - Philippines bilateral Hormuz deal helps shipping but not fertilizer access. India export ban in place. Apr 7 deadline tonight; 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait reinforces no near-term resolution.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Gulf condensate-derived naphtha holding elevated as Brent near four-year highs ahead of Apr 7 deadline. Tehran Toll Booth active; Qatar force majeure unresolved; 15 US soldiers injured Kuwait + IDF facility strikes remove near-term supply restoration path. Jurong Island and Map Ta Phut crackers remain at reduced rates. Source: SGX NJF1 futures.

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Downstream from naphtha holding near highs. SABIC and QAPCO ethylene exports still bottlenecked; naphtha cost pass-through continuing with a lag. SE Asian PE and PVC producers see marginal cost reversal; agricultural film and irrigation pipe lead times still 6-8 weeks. Apr 7 deadline tonight; tail risk 56%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia.

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Iran is world's 2nd largest methanol exporter - production disrupted directly by conflict. Qatar force majeure in effect. China MTO complex facing feedstock squeeze. IDF strikes Iran's largest facility Apr 6 further delays production restart. Apr 7 deadline tonight adds uncertainty; partial offset from US and Chinese domestic supply. Source: Investing.com CFR China futures.

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Qafco (Qatar) force majeure in effect since Mar 2. Saudi SAFCO and SABIC urea plants at ~60% capacity. 15 US soldiers injured Kuwait + IDF strikes Iran's largest facility eliminate production restart path. Tehran Toll Booth active - Hormuz access structurally uncertain. Egypt and India panic-buying from Russia/China at large premiums; Apr 7 deadline raises acute risk of further Saudi capacity cuts. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers.

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: Saudi Ma'aden DAP shipping disrupted; Toll Booth transit cost adds $20-30/mt delivered premium. 15 US soldiers injured Kuwait + IDF strikes Iran's largest facility raise Saudi output-cut risk further. US Gulf filling gap at premium freight. Philippines and Vietnam facing import cost spikes ahead of wet-season planting; Apr 7 deadline binary.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Less directly Hormuz-exposed (Canada, Russia main suppliers) but elevated freight costs add indirect pressure. Apr 7 deadline tonight; escalation maintains war-risk freight premium. Palm oil plantations (Indonesia, Malaysia) are major users - any yield disruption from fertilizer shortage amplifies CPO supply concern.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: Cape re-routing absorbing dry bulk vessel capacity as iron ore, coal, and grain shipments divert around southern Africa. Australia-Japan coal routing Cape adding 12 days. Apr 7 deadline + 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait confirms no near-term Hormuz routing recovery - vessels committed to Cape through at least Q2. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News.

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: Tehran Toll Booth active ($2M/vessel in yuan); VLCC owners face dual pricing structure. 15 US soldiers injured Kuwait + IDF strikes Iran's largest facility add pre-deadline risk premium. P&I restoration impossible until political settlement. ~35% of VLCC fleet war-risk excluded; Kharg Island seizure risk premium surging ahead of Apr 7 8pm ET deadline. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List.

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: Asia-Europe container rates holding elevated ahead of Apr 7 deadline; no Suez Canal restoration path. Kuwait damage ongoing; 15 US soldiers injured in Kuwait Apr 6. Philippines bilateral Hormuz deal offers marginal relief for Manila-Gulf routes only. SE Asian manufacturers still facing extended lead times. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange.
